methods.description: Process for excavating test pits and collecting samples for analysis, focusing on natural stratigraphy and faunal composition.

steps.description: Four test pits were excavated between 1985 and 2003. All excavations were done with a trowel and small metal shovel.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate test pits using trowel and shovel

steps.description: The first and deepest test pit was excavated in 1985 and measured 1 m length by 1m width and reached over 1 m in depth.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate first test pit with specified dimensions

steps.description: The second had a similar measurement, but only 50 cm in depth.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate second test pit with specified dimensions

steps.description: The last two test pits measured 50 cm x 50 cm x 50 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate last two test pits with specified dimensions

steps.description: These test pits followed 10 cm intervals with attention to the natural stratigraphy.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate in 10 cm intervals considering stratigraphy

steps.description: Unconformities and erosional surfaces were detected from excavation profiles.
All analyzed material was extracted in situ from the lateral profile into a glass vial.
steps.searchable_summary: extract material in situ into glass vial

methods.description: Procedure for processing excavated material, including sieving, subsampling, and species diversity calculation.

steps.description: The excavated material was dry sieved with a fine screen mesh (0.3 cm).
steps.searchable_summary: dry sieve material with 0.3 cm mesh

steps.description: From each sieved sample, a subsample collection was randomly placed in plastic bottles (~462 cm³).
This was later softly dry brushed in the lab to remove adhered matrix and soil.
steps.searchable_summary: dry brush subsamples to remove matrix and soil

steps.description: Material separated following Silva Taboada (1974) but including juveniles and other parts of the appendicular skeleton in the tallies following the method described in Orihuela (2010).
steps.searchable_summary: separate material and tally species diversity
